rag_rules: Use initialized temporal recurrence for pre() expressions, e.g., initialized : bool = false -> true, and guard previous-value comparisons on initialized.
Ensure type consistency in binary expressions; resolve '<error>' types by fixing root reference issues or removing invalid clauses.
Place AGREE contracts in annex agree blocks within valid component type or implementation scopes.
Declare visible helpers and references in the same annex scope to avoid 'Couldn't resolve reference' errors.
generic_rules: Use initialized state variables to guard pre() expression comparisons in AGREE contracts.
Validate type consistency in AGREE expressions to prevent '<error>' type mismatches.
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when referring to ports or features.
Only reference visible identifiers or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to avoid resolution errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use `->` for initialized temporal recurrence in stateful equations.
Binary expressions in AGREE must have matching types; resolve type mismatches by aligning declarations.
Pre() expressions must be guarded by initialized flags to avoid undefined behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not reference unresolved identifiers or invalid helpers in AGREE guarantees or equations.
Avoid placing component-level assumptions or guarantees in implementation scopes unless binding to subcomponents.
Do not mix incompatible types (e.g., int and real) in binary expressions without explicit conversion.
Do not declare AGREE helpers or constants in scopes where they are not visible to the current annex.
irrelevant_topics: Component type annexes vs implementation annexes
AGREE logical operators and implication syntax
